candidates.title: Phase Transitions and Finite-Size Scaling in SAE Feature Absorption
candidates.summary: SAE absorption exhibits quasi-critical threshold behavior at λ_c≈5e-5 with finite-size scaling (ν=3, R²=0.951). Most surprisingly, absorbed features show 733x higher CV than non-absorbed features (7.33 vs 0.01), suggesting absorption selectively preserves context-sensitive high-variance information rather than uniformly degrading signal. The phase transition is gradual (chi_ratio=1.88 < 3.0), not sharp.
hypotheses: H1: Quasi-critical threshold at λ_c≈5e-5 with susceptibility peak
H2: Finite-size scaling with ν≈3 and R²>0.95
H4: Variance paradox - absorbed features have HIGHER CV (discovery, not failure)
candidates.pilot_focus: Cross-layer measurement at λ_c=5e-5 using SAEBench probe projection; CV decomposition validation; steering test for high-CV vs low-CV absorbed features
strengths: First finite-size scaling measurement in SAE literature (ν=3, R²=0.951)
Genuine discovery (CV reversal) requiring new theoretical explanation
Phase transition framework is novel framing not in prior work
weaknesses: chi_ratio=1.88 below sharp transition threshold of 3.0

candidates.title: Projection-Based Cross-Layer Absorption Quantification
candidates.summary: Use SAEBench probe projection metric to measure absorption across all layers without ablation. Cross-layer absorption patterns can be characterized without layer-specific saturation issues that plagued H3 at λ=0.001.
hypotheses: H3 (refined): Cross-layer absorption heterogeneity at λ_c
candidates.pilot_focus: Layers 0,3,6,9,11 at λ_c=5e-5 using SAEBench probe projection metric
strengths: No ablation required - works across all layers
Publishable in either direction (variation found or not)
weaknesses: If no cross-layer variation found even at λ_c, backup also fails
Still needs validation at true critical sparsity

candidates.title: Steering Effectiveness and Actionability Analysis
candidates.summary: Test whether absorption metrics predict steering effectiveness. Addresses Basu et al. actionability paradox directly: does good detection (high AUROC) translate to steering utility? Tests CV-based hypothesis that high-CV absorbed features may retain steering potential.
hypotheses: CV predicts steering effectiveness (high-CV absorbed features steerable)
candidates.pilot_focus: Steering test on 30 high-CV vs 30 low-CV absorbed features; absorbed vs non-absorbed steering comparison
strengths: Extends Basu et al. to non-clinical domain
Tests actionable hypothesis from CV discovery
weaknesses: If universal actionability failure confirmed, negative result may be too strong

candidates.title: Cross-Architecture Phase Transition Validation
candidates.summary: Validate that finite-size scaling (ν=3) discovered on GPT-2 TopK SAEs generalizes to Gemma-2-2B JumpReLU SAEs. Tests artifact hypothesis: are phase transitions GPT-2/TopK-specific or universal?
hypotheses: ν=3 is universal across SAE types
candidates.pilot_focus: GPT-2 sparsity sweep (confirm λ_c=5e-5); Gemma-2 sparsity sweep (identify λ_c); scaling collapse comparison
strengths: First cross-architecture validation of phase transition in SAEs
weaknesses: If Gemma-2 shows no phase transition, artifact hypothesis confirmed
λ_c variation requires additional theoretical explanation

metadata.prior_feedback: Result debate verdict: 5.5/10, PIVOT recommendation, chi_ratio below threshold, H3/H6 falsified
